title: Jonathan Jacob Adessky
content:
## Jonathan Jacob Adessky
People.ContactEmail jadessky@mccarthy.ca
Jonathan Adessky is an associate in the firm’s Business Law Group. His practice focuses on technology transactions, IT outsourcing and a wide range of cyber/data advisory and incident response matters. He is an active member of the firm’s national Technology Group and Cyber/Data Group. Jonathan maintains a sector‑agnostic practice, with particular experience in retail, fintech, automotive, critical infrastructure and digital entertainment. He brings deep subject‑matter expertise at the intersection of technology, data, and commercial and corporate transactions, and regularly advises on complex, cross‑border and mission‑critical technology arrangements. Jonathan was a contributing author to McCarthy Tétrault’s chapters in the Artificial Intelligence, Law Over Borders: Comparative Guide (2022 and 2024). He also launched and now co‑oversees the Cyber/Data Group’s Data Breach Insight Series.
- Advising a global engineering company on multiple IT outsourcing projects implemented across more than 20 countries worldwide;
- Advising operators of major critical infrastructure on complex technology contracts in the aviation, rail, and maritime sectors;
- Assisting a fintech in negotiating large‑scale BPO outsourcing agreements relating to mortgage origination and servicing;
- Advising domestic and international clients on digital transformation initiatives, including risk mapping, legal risk assessments, and negotiation of vendor and technology agreements;
- Assisting a major Canadian retailer negotiate a commercial agreement to build and maintain an automated distribution center;
- Drafting and negotiating a wide range of technology agreements, including custom and enterprise‑grade software development, licensing, SaaS, and related commercial arrangements; and
- Assisting on numerous M&A transactions, including supporting foreign acquirers in the acquisition of Canadian technology companies, with particular focus on privacy, data, and intellectual property matters.
- Advising organizations on the development of responsible AI governance strategies and on negotiating commercial agreements with AI developers and service providers;
- Routinely advising Canadian and multinational organizations on Canadian privacy compliance, including providing privacy-focused, pragmatic solutions to some of the world’s largest companies;
- Designing and helping implement end‑to‑end data governance programs, with a particular focus on compliance with Québec’s Law 25;
- Acting as breach coach for numerous clients, including in connection with high‑profile data breaches;
- Assisting clients with e‑commerce offerings, including drafting privacy policies and terms of use, and advising on electronic contracting and e‑signatures.
Jonathan holds a BCL/JD from McGill University’s Faculty of Law and a BComm (Finance) from Concordia University’s John Molson School of Business, including a semester at Universidad Carlos III in Madrid. He is certified by the IAPP as a Certified Information Privacy Professional for Canada and the European Union (CIPP‑C and CIPP‑E).
Outside of his legal practice, Jonathan served for approximately five years as an infantry reservist in the Canadian Forces and has performed professionally as a guitarist. His band appeared at the 2014 and 2015 editions of the Festival International de Jazz de Montréal.
Jonathan was called to the Québec Bar in 2021 and is a member of the Bar of Montréal.

Leading Lawyer: Privacy & Data Protection
